task_completion.description: Verify that the 'us_western_capitals' GeoJSON file is loaded into QGIS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'us_western_capitals' layer should be visible in the QGIS Layers panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Distance Matrix tool is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Distance Matrix' tool dialog window should be visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Distance Matrix tool is configured to compute a linear matrix for the nearest 1 target point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Distance Matrix dialog, 'Output matrix type' should be set to 'Linear (N*k x 3) distance matrix' and 'Use only the nearest (k) target points' should be set to 1. The input and target layers should both be 'us_western_capitals'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Distance Matrix tool has been executed successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new layer (typically named 'Distance matrix') should appear in the QGIS Layers panel, or a success message/log from the tool execution should be visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the resulting distance matrix is exported to the correct CSV file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Vector Layer as...' dialog showing the format as CSV and the file name as '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/capital_distance_matrix.csv', or a file manager/terminal showing the created file.

task_completion.description: Verify that the QGIS project is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QGIS window title should display 'distance_analysis' or a Save Project dialog should show the path '/home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/distance_analysis.qgz'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the QGIS graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the QGIS application windows, menus, and dialogs.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not manually type the CSV contents in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) being used to manually write the 'capital_distance_matrix.csv' file.

integrity.description: Verify that the distance matrix was generated by the QGIS tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the tool running in QGIS (progress bars, log messages, or the resulting layer in the Layers panel) should be present, rather than a script being run in the terminal to bypass QGIS.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input GeoJSON contains exactly 17 US state capitals. The expected output CSV should have 17 rows (excluding the header) representing the nearest neighbor for each capital. Known nearest neighbor pairs include Denver-Cheyenne, Bismarck-Pierre, Lincoln-Topeka, Sacramento-Carson City, and Salem-Olympia.
